questions: What is the correct approach to rewire runUpdate() and checkAutoUpdate() from npm-semver to SHA-based comparison?
How should performUpdate() install spec change, and does npm correctly handle global git+https installs (prepare script, package placement)?
How should the npm-to-GitHub migration path work within the existing update flow?
What is the correct way to extend getPackageVersion() to include commit SHA from config?
Should isCanonicalSource() change, and how does it interact with migration?
What functions/constants in check.ts should be removed vs kept after the switch?

answers.answer: runUpdate() should call fetchRemoteSha() directly (not isUpdateAvailable()) and check for null explicitly — null means fetch failure and must exit non-zero (fail-closed). If non-null, read the locally-recorded SHA from config.installSource.commit. If the SHAs match, print 'Already up to date'. If different or absent, run install. checkAutoUpdate() should use a similar pattern but call fetchRemoteSha() and silently return on null (non-blocking). Both flows replace fetchLatestVersion()/isNewerVersion() with fetchRemoteSha() + SHA string comparison.
evidence: src/update/index.ts:55-67 — runUpdate() currently calls fetchLatestVersion() and isNewerVersion(), must switch to fetchRemoteSha() + SHA comparison
src/update/index.ts:129-139 — checkAutoUpdate() uses same npm-based comparison
src/update/check.ts:54-68 — fetchRemoteSha() returns string|null, null means failure
src/update/check.ts:73-85 — isUpdateAvailable() returns {available:false, remoteSha:null} on failure — conflates 'up to date' with 'fetch error' for callers that need fail-closed behavior
Ticket: 'Remote SHA fetch fails: hlx update exits non-zero'; 'auto-update pre-command check logs a warning to stderr and continues'

answers.answer: performUpdate() should change the install spec from NPM_PACKAGE@latest to git+CANONICAL_REPO_URL#CANONICAL_BRANCH. A new constant (e.g. GIT_INSTALL_SPEC) should be exported from check.ts composed from existing constants. npm correctly handles global git+https installs: it clones the repo, installs devDependencies to run the prepare script (tsc), builds, and places the result under node_modules using the package name field. Since the name is @projectxinnovation/helix-cli, the global path is identical to registry installs. validateInstall() requires no changes.
evidence: src/update/perform.ts:15-17 — current spec is NPM_PACKAGE@latest
src/update/check.ts:3-5 — CANONICAL_REPO_URL = 'https://github.com/Project-X-Innovation/helix-cli.git', CANONICAL_BRANCH = 'main'
package.json:2 — name: '@projectxinnovation/helix-cli'
package.json:19 — prepare: 'npm run build' which runs tsc
npm docs (Context7): 'When installing from a Git repository, npm may perform a build if certain scripts (prepare) are present'; devDependencies example shows prepare-based builds are supported
src/update/validate.ts:41-47 — constructs path as globalRoot/@projectxinnovation/helix-cli/dist/index.js — works for git installs since npm uses the name field for directory placement

answers.answer: Migration is handled inline within the existing update flow, not as a separate code path. In runUpdate(): after fetching the remote SHA, check if config.installSource is missing or mode:'npm'. If so, print a one-line notice (e.g. 'Switching install source from npm to GitHub main...'). Then proceed with the normal install path — the install itself is identical whether it's a migration or a regular update. After success, record {mode:'github', repo, branch, commit}. In checkAutoUpdate(): same detection, but no printed notice (quiet mode). When installSource.commit is absent (npm-sourced), any remote SHA is treated as 'different' and triggers the install. This naturally handles the migration case.
evidence: src/update/index.ts:93-98 — currently saves {mode:'npm', version: remoteVersion}; migration changes this to {mode:'github', repo, branch, commit}
src/lib/config.ts:5-11 — InstallSource type already supports mode:'github' with repo/branch/commit fields

Ticket: 'First-run migration: when hlx update runs and the recorded install source is missing or marked as npm, the command must (a) print a one-line notice, (b) run the install path, (c) record the install source as GitHub with the new SHA'
Ticket: 'Config file is unreadable or corrupt: treat as no recorded install source, trigger the migration path'

answers.answer: getPackageVersion() should be extended to call loadFullConfig() and read installSource.commit. If commit is a non-empty string, return '<semver> (<commit.slice(0,7)>)'. If commit is missing (legacy npm installs or fresh installs before first hlx update), return the semver only. The --version handler at src/index.ts:124 already calls getPackageVersion() and prints directly, so no change is needed there. A separate concern: fresh installs (npm install -g git+https://...) do not run hlx update, so the first hlx invocation has no SHA in config — the fallback to semver-only is correct, and the ticket specifies a note to run hlx update to refresh metadata.
evidence: src/update/version.ts:10-20 — getPackageVersion() reads semver from package.json, returns string
src/lib/config.ts:88-96 — loadFullConfig() reads raw config including installSource
src/index.ts:122-124 — --version prints getPackageVersion() directly

Ticket: 'hlx --version must include the short SHA of the installed commit, drawn from the same config field. If the field is missing (legacy installs), fall back to the existing semver-only output and a one-line note to run hlx update'

answers.answer: isCanonicalSource() should continue to accept mode:'npm' as canonical. This allows checkAutoUpdate() to proceed for npm-sourced users, triggering the silent migration. After migration, the installSource becomes mode:'github' which is already accepted. Removing mode:'npm' from isCanonicalSource would block auto-update for users who haven't explicitly run hlx update yet, creating a worse migration experience. No change needed to isCanonicalSource() — it already handles both modes correctly.
evidence: src/update/index.ts:20-28 — isCanonicalSource() accepts mode:'npm' and mode:'github' with matching repo/branch
src/update/index.ts:121-127 — checkAutoUpdate() gates on isCanonicalSource() before proceeding
Ticket: 'The pre-command auto-update check performs the same SHA comparison and triggers the same install path on mismatch, in quiet mode'

answers.answer: fetchLatestVersion() and isNewerVersion() in check.ts should be removed — they are npm-registry-specific functions that will have zero callers after the switch. The NPM_PACKAGE constant should be removed from check.ts since it's no longer needed for the install spec. A new GIT_INSTALL_SPEC constant should be added, composed from existing CANONICAL_REPO_URL and CANONICAL_BRANCH. The existing fetchRemoteSha(), isUpdateAvailable(), CANONICAL_REPO_URL, CANONICAL_BRANCH, and CANONICAL_REPO constants should be kept. Note: NPM_PACKAGE is currently imported by perform.ts, so removing it requires updating that import to use GIT_INSTALL_SPEC instead.
evidence: src/update/check.ts:8-9 — NPM_PACKAGE constant, imported by perform.ts
src/update/check.ts:15-29 — fetchLatestVersion() used only by index.ts runUpdate/checkAutoUpdate
src/update/check.ts:36-47 — isNewerVersion() used only by index.ts runUpdate/checkAutoUpdate
src/update/perform.ts:2 — imports NPM_PACKAGE from check.ts

statement: The update system rewiring is a well-contained change within the src/update/ module plus documentation surfaces. The existing git primitives (fetchRemoteSha, isUpdateAvailable, CANONICAL_* constants, InstallSource.commit config field) provide the foundation — they need to be wired into the update flows that currently use npm-registry functions. The key technical decisions are: (1) runUpdate() calls fetchRemoteSha() directly with explicit null-check for fail-closed behavior; (2) performUpdate() uses a new GIT_INSTALL_SPEC constant; (3) install source recording switches to {mode:'github', repo, branch, commit}; (4) migration is inline within the normal update flow with a conditional notice; (5) getPackageVersion() reads SHA from config; (6) npm-specific functions and the NPM_PACKAGE constant are removed from check.ts. validateInstall() and isCanonicalSource() require no changes. 12 documentation and error-message locations across 7 files need text updates to reference the git+https URL.
